title: Laptop and Mobile Components, Replacement, and Physical Service · Guided Service Studio
summary: A guided support bench extends mobile hardware serviceability and repair decision while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of mobile hardware serviceability and repair decision while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map batteries, displays, digitizers, cameras, microphones, speakers, keyboards, touchpads, antennas, storage, memory, wireless cards, ports, and replaceable units.
Use exact model identity, service documentation, fastener and cable maps, tool requirements, warranty, parts, and reassembly checkpoints.
Trace adapter, cable, negotiated power, port, charge controller, battery state, firmware, thermal conditions, and operating-system reporting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Mobile Component Map
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The workshop variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Map batteries, displays, digitizers, cameras, microphones, speakers, keyboards, touchpads, antennas, storage, memory, wireless cards, ports, and replaceable units.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Mobile Component Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map batteries, displays, digitizers, cameras, microphones, speakers, keyboards, touchpads, antennas, storage, memory, wireless cards, ports, and replaceable units. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Mobile Component Map, keep Mobile Component Map connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Reassembly Verification, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Reassembly Verification precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Reassembly Verification, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The workshop variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify fit, cable routing, fasteners, enclosure, charge, thermal state, keyboard, touch, audio, camera, wireless, ports, sleep, and data access.

For CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Reassembly Verification, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Successful boot after reassembly proves the physical repair is complete.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Reassembly Verification
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The workshop variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify fit, cable routing, fasteners, enclosure, charge, thermal state, keyboard, touch, audio, camera, wireless, ports, sleep, and data access.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Reassembly Verification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify fit, cable routing, fasteners, enclosure, charge, thermal state, keyboard, touch, audio, camera, wireless, ports, sleep, and data access. content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Reassembly Verification symptom.

content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

stimulus.title: Reassembly Verification failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Successful boot after reassembly proves the physical repair is complete.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify fit, cable routing, fasteners, enclosure, charge, thermal state, keyboard, touch, audio, camera, wireless, ports, sleep, and data access.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Reassembly Verification condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Reassembly Verification.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend the transferred model · Workshop Reassembly Verification
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Reassembly Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify fit, cable routing, fasteners, enclosure, charge, thermal state, keyboard, touch, audio, camera, wireless, ports, sleep, and data access.
